source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
DESCRIPTION

As an Account Executive, you will play a crucial role in driving revenue growth by cultivating and expanding relationships with clients. As a key member of the sales team, you will leverage your exceptional communication skills, strategic mindset, and customer-centric approach to identify opportunities, close deals, and exceed revenue targets. This role is ideal for individuals who thrive in a dynamic and collaborative environment and are passionate about delivering value to clients.



Job Responsibilities

 - Sales Calls and Demos: Conduct effective sales calls and product demonstrations to showcase the features, benefits, and value propositions of the product or service. Use persuasive communication skills to address prospect questions and objections, and highlight how the offering can meet their needs.

 - Relationship Building: Build and maintain strong relationships with prospects, understanding their businesses, challenges, and goals. Establish yourself and the company as a trusted advisor through effective communication and rapport building.

 - Qualify leads: Assess and qualify leads to determine their potential as viable business opportunities. Understand the prospect's requirements, pain points, and budget to determine the fit with the company's products or services.

 - Sales Pipeline Management: Track and manage sales opportunities using CRM software. Keep accurate and up-to-date records of all interactions, activities, and progress with prospects. Prioritize leads and manage your time effectively to meet sales targets.

 - Sales Negotiation and Closing: Employ strong negotiation skills to create win-win situations and close deals. Understand prospect budgets, timelines, and decision-making processes to navigate through the sales cycle and secure contracts.

 - Collaboration: Collaborate closely with the success team, marketing team, and other relevant stakeholders to ensure a smooth handover of qualified leads and maintain a cohesive sales strategy.

 - Market Research: Stay updated on industry trends, competitor activities, and market conditions. Provide insights and feedback to the marketing and product teams to improve the sales approach and optimize the product offering.


REQUIREMENTS

 - At least 2 years experience as a Business Development Representative or a similar sales role, preferably in a B2B (business-to-business) environment.

 - Strong sales and negotiation skills, with a track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.

 - Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage and build relationships with diverse stakeholders and to effectively articulate complex concepts in a simple and concise manner.

 - Self-motivated and goal-oriented, with the ability to work independently and as part of a team.

 - Familiarity with CRM software and sales tools to manage leads and sales activities.

 - Ability to quickly understand customer needs and tailor the sales approach accordingly.

 - Good organizational and time management skills, with the ability to prioritize tasks effectively.

 - Strong problem-solving and critical-thinking abilities.



Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:

 - Internet Connection:
   
   - Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of 15 Mbps.
   
   - Backup internet connection with at least 10 Mbps.
   
   - Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage.



 - Primary Device:
   
   - Desktop or laptop equipped with at least:
     
     - Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer), Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer), AMD Ryzen 5, or an equivalent processor.
     
     - A minimum of 8 GB RAM.



 - Backup Device:
   
   - Must meet or exceed the performance of an Intel Core i3 processor.
   
   - Must be functional during power interruptions.



 - A functioning webcam.

 - A noise-canceling USB headset.

 - A quiet, dedicated home office space.

 - Peripherals and Workspace: A smartphon

source_jobs.title: Graphic Designer
source_jobs.description:
DESCRIPTION

We’re looking for a creative and detail-oriented GraphicDesigner to support one of our clients. In this fully remote role, you’ll design impactful marketing assets and user-friendly web experiences that elevate the brand and engage audiences.

You’ll collaborate closely with marketing and cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality visuals across digital platforms while maintaining strong brand consistency.




JOB RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Design engaging marketing materials (social media graphics, banners, brochures, ads)

 - Create clean, intuitive web and landing page designs

 - Develop and maintain consistent brand visuals across platforms

 - Conduct basic user research and apply UX best practices

 - Manage multiple projects while meeting deadlines


REQUIREMENTS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Graphic Design or related field

 - 3+ years of graphic design experience with a strong portfolio

 - 2+ years of web design experience

 - Proven ability to create ad sets and one-page websites on time

 - Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite, Sketch, Adobe XD, or similar tools

 - Strong understanding of UX principles and modern web design trends

 - Excellent communication skills and ability to work independently



Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:

 - Internet Connection:
   
   - Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of 15 Mbps.
   
   - Backup internet connection with at least 10 Mbps.
   
   - Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage.



 - Primary Device:
   
   - Desktop or laptop equipped with at least:
     
     - Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer), Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer), AMD Ryzen 5, or an equivalent processor.
     
     - A minimum of 8 GB RAM.



 - Backup Device:
   
   - Must meet or exceed the performance of an Intel Core i3 processor.
   
   - Must be functional during power interruptions.



 - A functioning webcam.

 - A noise-canceling USB headset.

 - A quiet, dedicated home office space.

 - Peripherals and Workspace: A smartphone for communication and verification purposes.


BENEFITS

 - Join Our Dynamic Team: Experience our fun, inclusive, innovative culture that values your unique contributions and supports your professional growth.

 - Embrace the Opportunities: Seize daily chances to learn, innovate, and excel. Make a real impact in your field.

 - Limitless Career Growth: Unlock a world of possibilities and resources to propel your career forward.

 - Fast-Paced Thrills: Thrive in a high-energy, engaging atmosphere. Embrace challenges and reap stimulating rewards.

 - Flexibility, Your Way: Embrace the freedom to work from home or any location of your choice. Create your ideal work environment.

 - Work-Life Balance at Its Best: Say goodbye to stressful commutes and hello to quality time with loved ones. Achieve a healthy work-life integration to perform at your best.

source_jobs.title: Senior Business Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

At CrewBloom, we aren’t looking for a "task-taker"—we are looking for a growth architect.

The Senior Business Development Representative is a high-leverage, operator-level position designed to act as an extension of the leadership team. Your primary mission is to aggressively expand our market footprint while reclaiming founder bandwidth. You won't just follow a script; you will own the end-to-end sales engine, optimize our go-to-market strategy, and execute with the high agency required to turn cold prospects into long-term partners.

Important note: This is a fully-remote role.




WHAT YOU’LL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR (OUTCOMES)

We measure success by impact, not activity. You will be expected to deliver the following:

 - Founder Leverage & Execution: Act as a strategic filter for leadership, managing the entire top-of-funnel and mid-funnel process so that founders only engage at the final "signature" stage

 - Revenue Engine Optimization: Move beyond "making calls" to "building systems." You will refine our pitch, iterate on sales collateral, and experiment with new outreach channels to increase conversion rates

 - High-Stakes Relationship Management: Position yourself as a consultant, not a vendor. You will diagnose complex business pain points and architect solutions that align with the prospect's long-term ROI

 - Pipeline Integrity: Maintain a high-velocity, high-accuracy CRM environment. You are responsible for the health of the sales forecast, ensuring data is a reliable source of truth for executive decision-making

 - Market Intelligence & Feedback Loops: Synthesize market resistance and competitor shifts into actionable insights for the Product and Marketing teams, directly influencing our roadmap




WHAT YOU’VE DONE BEFORE

 - 3+ Years in B2B Growth: You have a proven track record of navigating complex B2B sales cycles, specifically within the tech or services industry

 - Closed Meaningful Business: You can point to specific instances where you didn’t just hit a quota, but exceeded it by identifying untapped market segments

 - Mastered the Tech Stack: You have advanced proficiency in CRM (Salesforce/HubSpot) and modern sales acceleration tools (Apollo, Outreach, etc.)

 - Strategic Problem Solving: You’ve successfully turned "No" into "Not yet" or "Yes" through creative negotiation and value-based selling, rather than aggressive discounting




WHO YOU ARE

 - A High-Agency Operator: You don't wait for a manual. When you see a gap in a process, you fix it

 - An Expert Communicator: You can distill complex value propositions into punchy, persuasive narratives that resonate with C-suite executives

 - Data-Informed: You respect the numbers. You use conversion data to decide where to spend your next hour for maximum impact

 - Resilient & Proactive: You view objections as data points and setbacks as opportunities to pivot the strategy




WHO THIS IS NOT FOR

 - The "Clock-In" Mentality: If you are looking for a routine 9-to-5 where you wait for instructions, this isn't the fit.

 - Administrative Specialists: If your primary skill is data entry rather than deal-making and strategic thinking, you will find the pace overwhelming.

 - The Script-Dependent: If you cannot navigate a conversation without a pre-written guide, you will struggle with our high-level clientele.




WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

 - In 30 Days: You have mastered the CrewBloom value proposition, audited our current outreach sequence, and identified three immediate areas for optimization.

 - In 90 Days: You are consistently hitting pipeline targets, have successfully shortened the average sales cycle by 10%, and have offloaded 80% of initial sales discovery from the leadership team.

 - In 1 Year: You have built a repeatable, scalable outbound "playbook" that serves as the foundation for the next phase of our sales team's growth.


REQUIREMENTS

Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:

 - Internet Connection:

source_jobs.title: Sales Specialist
source_jobs.description:
SALES SPECIALIST




DESCRIPTION

We are hiring a Sales Specialist to win and scale multi-million euro partnerships with global F&B brands by selling beekeeping projects that turn pollination into measurable commercial value. This is high-stakes, high-impact work where you will engage directly with sustainability leaders at the world’s largest food companies, translating ecological impact into "boardroom-ready" business cases that drive Net Zero and regenerative agriculture targets.

To succeed, you must become the definitive expert: mastering the internal networks of global agribusinesses, navigating complex supply chain challenges, and speaking the language of Science-Based Targets and Nature Positive goals to convert interest into high-ROI action.



Job Responsibilities

Build Strategic Relationships

 - Pinpoint and connect with key decision-makers—from ESG Directors to farm managers—within global F&B and agribusiness sectors

 - Bridge the gap between internal silos by mapping stakeholders across supply chain, marketing, and procurement

 - Expand reach through peer referrals and establish a reputation as a thought leader



Prospect with Precision

 - Spot the "execution gap" in ESG reporting where corporate goals haven't yet met measurable action

 - Map key focus areas such as regenerative agriculture and supply chain transparency

 - Assess budget cycles and procurement workflows to ensure a fast-tracked decision-making process

Sell Through Impact

 - Quantify pollination as a driver for biodiversity, traceability, and carbon goals

 - Bridge the gap between corporate environmental goals and operational risk reduction

 - Ensure our offerings are recognized as validated contributions toward corporate Nature Positive and Science-Based Target goals

 - Pivot from listening to execution by diagnosing sourcing hurdles and delivering high-ROI proposals with concrete, phased timelines


REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years of sales experience (preferably in agribusiness or food & beverage sectors)

 - Proven track record prospecting and closing complex, multi-stakeholder accounts

 - Experience navigating long sales cycles and building business cases for sustainability initiatives

 - Exceptional at identifying and engaging sustainability decision-makers

 - Strong business case development—you help internal champions secure buy-in and budget

 - Hands-on with CRM systems (HubSpot or Salesforce), pricing tools, and analytics (Sheets/Excel)

 - Outstanding communication skills—you translate technical impact into compelling narratives

 - Entrepreneurial mindset with resilience and persistence

 - Hard worker who can manage multiple complex accounts simultaneously

 - Realistic judgment about when sustainability interest converts to budget commitment

 - Genuine passion for environmental impact (not just a buzzword)

 - Fluent English required; French, Spanish, Portuguese a strong advantage



Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:

 - Internet Connection:
   
   - Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of 15 Mbps.
   
   - Backup internet connection with at least 10 Mbps.
   
   - Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage.



 - Primary Device:
   
   - Desktop or laptop equipped with at least:
     
     - Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer), Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer), AMD Ryzen 5, or an equivalent processor.
     
     - A minimum of 8 GB RAM.



 - Backup Device:
   
   - Must meet or exceed the performance of an Intel Core i3 processor.
   
   - Must be functional during power interruptions.



 - Peripherals and Workspace:
   
   - A functioning webcam.
   
   - A noise-canceling USB headset.
   
   - A quiet, dedicated home office space.
   
   - A smartphone for communication and verification purposes.


BENEFITS

Join Our Dynamic Team: Experience our fun, inclusive, innovative culture that values your unique contributions and supports your professiona

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
DESCRIPTION

As an Account Executive, you will drive revenue growth by managing the full end-to-end sales cycle—from lead generation to closing deals. You will work closely with prospects to understand their business needs, present tailored solutions, and build lasting client relationships.

This role requires strong B2B sales experience, excellent communication skills, and the ability to handle enterprise or large corporate accounts. If you thrive in a fast-paced, goal-driven environment and enjoy delivering value to clients, this position is a great fit.






Job Responsibilities

 - Conduct effective sales calls and product demonstrations to highlight key features, benefits, and value propositions. Address prospect questions, handle objections, and demonstrate how the offering solves their business needs.

 - Build and maintain strong prospect and client relationships. Understand their business challenges, goals, and workflows. Establish credibility and position the company as a trusted advisor.

 - Qualify inbound and outbound leads by assessing business fit, pain points, budget, and buying intent. Ensure only viable opportunities enter the sales pipeline.

 - Manage and track opportunities using CRM tools. Maintain accurate, updated records of all engagements and activities. Prioritize leads and manage time effectively to meet or exceed sales targets.

 - Use strong negotiation skills to drive win-win agreements. Understand budgets, decision-making processes, and timelines to successfully navigate the sales cycle and close business.

 - Partner closely with Customer Success, Marketing, and Product teams to ensure seamless handoff of accounts and alignment across the sales strategy.

 - Stay informed about industry trends, competitor offerings, and market conditions. Provide insights to internal teams to continuously refine sales strategies and product positioning.


REQUIREMENTS

 - Must have end-to-end sales experience — from lead generation, prospecting, pitching, negotiating, to closing business.

 - Must have strong experience in the B2B market, preferably selling to SMBs, mid-market, or enterprise accounts.

 - Good to excellent English communication skills (verbal and written) are required.

 - Must have experience handling enterprise accounts or large corporate clients in previous roles.



Other Requirements:

 - At least 2 years of experience as an Account Executive, Business Development Representative, or similar quota-carrying sales role.

 - Proven sales and negotiation skills, with a track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets.

 - Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to build rapport and communicate complex concepts simply and compellingly.

 - Self-motivated, competitive, and goal-oriented, with the ability to work independently and within a team.

 - Familiarity with CRM systems and sales tools for managing pipelines and activities.

 - Ability to understand customer pain points and tailor the sales approach accordingly.

 - Strong organizational and time-management skills.

 - Excellent problem-solving and critical-thinking abilities.



Minimum Technical and Work Environment Requirements:

 - Internet Connection:
   
   - Primary internet connection with a minimum speed of 15 Mbps.
   
   - Backup internet connection with at least 10 Mbps.
   
   - Backup connection must be capable of supporting work during a power outage.



 - Primary Device:
   
   - Desktop or laptop equipped with at least:
     
     - Intel Core i5 (8th generation or newer), Intel Core i3 (10th generation or newer), AMD Ryzen 5, or an equivalent processor.
     
     - A minimum of 8 GB RAM.



 - Backup Device:
   
   - Must meet or exceed the performance of an Intel Core i3 processor.
   
   - Must be functional during power interruptions.



 - A functioning webcam.

 - A noise-canceling USB headset.

 - A quiet, dedicated home office space.

 - Peripherals and Workspace: A smartphone for communication and verifi

source_jobs.title: Sales Director
source_jobs.description:
WHY THIS ROLE EXISTS

We are looking for a Growth Manager who operates like a revenue operator, not a traditional manager.

Our founders need someone who can build and scale a predictable growth engine—owning the systems, strategy, and execution that drive revenue across the sales funnel.

This role exists to increase founder leverage, improve execution speed, and build scalable systems for lead generation, pipeline growth, and deal conversion.

You will work directly with leadership to translate growth strategy into measurable revenue outcomes, using data, automation, and AI-driven workflows to continuously improve performance.

 


WHAT YOU’LL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR (OUTCOMES)


REVENUE GROWTH & PIPELINE GENERATION

Own the systems that generate and convert pipeline across inbound and outbound channels.

You will ensure that the company maintains a consistent, high-quality pipeline that supports revenue targets and growth goals.

Success metrics

 - Pipeline coverage (3–5x revenue target)

 - Lead-to-opportunity conversion rate

 - Monthly and quarterly revenue growth

 


AI-DRIVEN GROWTH SYSTEMS

Design and implement AI-powered workflows that increase productivity across lead generation, outreach, follow-ups, and sales analytics.

You will continuously test and optimize tools that automate repetitive work and allow the team to focus on high-value activities.

Success metrics

 - Reduced manual sales tasks

 - Increased outreach productivity

 - Faster response and follow-up cycles

 


ICP REFINEMENT & MARKET POSITIONING

Continuously refine the ideal customer profile (ICP) to ensure growth efforts focus on the highest-value opportunities.

You will work closely with marketing and sales to align target markets, messaging, and positioning with real market demand.

Success metrics

 - Higher quality inbound leads

 - Improved meeting-to-close conversion rates

 - Shorter sales cycles

 


SALES PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION

Monitor performance across the entire pipeline and implement improvements to increase conversion and deal velocity.

You will use data and AI insights to identify bottlenecks and continuously improve the sales process.

Success metrics

 - Increased pipeline conversion rates

 - Forecast accuracy

 - Reduced sales cycle time

 


CROSS-FUNCTIONAL GROWTH EXECUTION

Collaborate closely with founders, marketing, and operations to ensure growth initiatives are aligned with company objectives.

You will help turn strategic initiatives into clear execution plans that move the business forward quickly.

Success metrics

 - Faster execution of growth initiatives

 - Improved alignment between marketing, sales, and operations

 - Consistent reporting and actionable insights for leadership

 


WHAT YOU’VE DONE BEFORE

 - Led or supported B2B growth or sales initiatives with measurable revenue impact.

 - Built and managed pipeline generation systems across inbound and outbound channels.

 - Used AI tools or automation to improve sales or growth workflows.

 - Operated in startup or fast-growth environments where adaptability and speed are critical.

 - Worked with CRM platforms (HubSpot, Salesforce, or similar) to track pipeline and performance.

 - Contributed to ICP development, messaging, and go-to-market strategy.

Experience in staffing, outsourcing, or remote talent solutions is a strong plus.

 


WHO YOU ARE

 - You think in systems, leverage, and outcomes, not tasks.

 - You are data-driven and operationally strong.

 - You are comfortable building and improving processes from scratch.

 - You thrive in fast-moving environments where execution speed matters.

 - You proactively identify growth opportunities and operational bottlenecks.

 - You take ownership of results, not just activity metrics.

 


WHO THIS IS NOT FOR

This role is not for someone who only executes tasks or follows predefined playbooks.

It is not for someone who:

 - Avoids accountability for growth outcomes

 - Is uncomfortable working with

source_jobs.title: Human Resource Generalist
source_jobs.description:
DESCRIPTION

The Human Resources Generalist is responsible for managing the full employee lifecycle for all office staff from sourcing and recruitment through onboarding, credentialing, active employment administration, and offboarding. This role is the operational backbone of administrative HR ensuring that every office employee is hired compliantly, onboarded thoroughly, credentialed before Day 1, tracked through their probationary period, maintained accurately in the HRIS and timekeeping systems, and separated properly when employment ends.

 

Office Staff Recruitment & Hiring

• Source qualified candidates for all open office staff positions through job boards, professional networks, referrals, and targeted outreach

• Post and manage job listings across active recruiting platforms and maintain accurate job descriptions for each office role

• Conduct initial phone screens to evaluate candidate qualifications, cultural fit, and availability

• Schedule and coordinate interviews between candidates and hiring managers or the HR Director

• Manage offer communications, pre-employment documentation collection, and hiring decisions in alignment with HR policy

• Maintain an active and organized candidate pipeline for all open office positions and provide weekly updates to the HR Director

 

New Hire Onboarding

• Execute the full onboarding process for all incoming office staff from offer acceptance through completion of the first week

• Ensure all pre-employment requirements are completed and verified before the employee's first day, including credentials, I-9 documentation, E-Verify, and signed offer materials

• Facilitate new hire orientation, ensuring employees understand policies, systems, expectations, and culture

• Coordinate with IT, operations, and department managers to ensure workstation access, system logins, and equipment are ready on Day 1

• Track onboarding completion for each new hire and follow up on any outstanding items until fully resolved

• Serve as the primary point of contact for new hire questions during the onboarding period

 

Fingerprint Clock-In System Administration

• Enroll all incoming office staff in the fingerprint clock-in system on or before their first day of employment

• Verify that each employee's fingerprint profile is active, functional, and linked to their correct HRIS record before their first shift

• Troubleshoot and resolve fingerprint enrollment issues promptly in coordination with IT or the system administrator

• Process clock-in system deactivations for separated employees on the same day as their termination effective date

• Maintain accurate records of all active fingerprint clock-in enrollments and flag any discrepancies to the HR Director

 

Credentialing & Pre-Employment Compliance

• Identify and collect all required credentials, certifications, and licenses for each office role before the employee's start date

• Verify the authenticity and validity of all submitted credentials before the employee begins work

• Maintain a credential tracking system for all office staff that includes issue dates, expiration dates, and renewal deadlines

• Send proactive renewal reminders to office staff in advance of any credential expiration

• Escalate any credential gaps, delays, or verification issues to the HR Director immediately

• Ensure zero office staff members begin employment with incomplete or unverified credentials

 

E-Verify Administration

• Complete E-Verify submissions for all office staff new hires within 3 business days of the date of hire

• Document all submission dates, case numbers, and final E-Verify statuses in the employee's personnel file

• Manage Tentative Non-Confirmation (TNC) cases in accordance with federal guidelines and escalate to the HR Director immediately upon receipt

• Maintain 100% E-Verify compliance across all office staff hires with no exceptions or missed submissions

 

Probation Period Tracking

• Maintain a current and accurat

source_jobs.title: Intake Specialist (Spanish Bilingual)
source_jobs.description:
DESCRIPTION

The Intake Specialist supports the full intake cycle for prospective ABA therapy clients, serving as a key point of contact for families, referring providers, and internal departments throughout enrollment. Working under the direction of the Lead Intake Specialist, this role is responsible for capturing and processing new referrals, verifying insurance eligibility, coordinating initial documentation, and supporting authorization workflows to move families from referral to active service as efficiently as possible. The ideal candidate is organized, compassionate, detail-oriented, and comfortable navigating healthcare insurance processes in a high-volume, regulated environment.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Referral Intake & Processing

 - Receive and log all incoming referrals via phone, fax, email, and referral portals in the intake tracking system accurately and in real time

 - Conduct initial eligibility screenings to verify insurance coverage, benefit levels, and ABA service authorization requirements

 - Gather and organize all required intake documentation from families and referring providers, including diagnostic evaluations, prescription letters, and consent forms

 - Communicate referral receipt and next-step instructions to families promptly and professionally

 - Escalate complex or high-priority referrals to the Lead Intake Specialist as appropriate

Authorization Support

 - Assist in the preparation and submission of prior authorization requests for ABA services to Medicaid, managed care organizations, and commercial insurance payers

 - Follow up on pending authorization submissions and document status updates in the tracking system

 - Coordinate with clinical staff (BCBAs / LBAs) to collect required clinical documentation in support of authorization requests

 - Flag cases that may require Single Case Agreement (SCA) processing and refer them to the Lead Intake Specialist or SCA Specialist

 - Monitor approaching authorization expiration dates and initiate re-authorization outreach under the direction of the Lead Intake Specialist

Family & Referral Source Communication

 - Contact families in a timely manner upon referral receipt to explain the intake process, collect required information, and address general inquiries

 - Provide empathetic and professional communication throughout the enrollment process, recognizing that families are often navigating a complex and unfamiliar system

 - Respond to inbound inquiries from families, pediatricians, schools, and other referral sources regarding intake status, required documents, and next steps

 - Ensure families are informed of any delays, documentation gaps, or payer-related requirements affecting their enrollment timeline

Documentation & Data Integrity

 - Maintain accurate, complete, and up-to-date records for all referrals and active intake cases in the designated tracking system

 - Ensure all intake files meet documentation standards required by payers, regulatory bodies

 - Conduct routine audits of assigned intake cases to identify missing documents, outstanding items, or stalled pipeline stages

 - Generate status reports on assigned caseload as requested by the Lead Intake Specialist or Director of Operations

Cross-Functional Coordination

 - Collaborate with the billing, clinical, and scheduling teams to ensure seamless handoff from intake authorization to active service delivery

 - Participate in intake team huddles, pipeline reviews, and cross-departmental coordination meetings as directed

 - Support the Lead Intake Specialist with special intake projects, payer-specific workflows, or capacity-driven assignments as needed

 - Adhere to all HIPAA regulations and confidentiality policies when handling client records and communications


REQUIREMENTS

 - High school diploma or equivalent required; Associate's or Bachelor's degree in Healthcare Administration, Human Services, Business, or a related field preferred

 - Minimum 1–2 years of exp
